items.title: Amazon EKS and Amazon EKS Distro now supports Kubernetes version 1.36
items.snippet: Kubernetes version 1.36 introduced several new features and bug fixes, and AWS is excited to announce that you can now use Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) and Amazon EKS Distro to run Kubernetes version 1.36. Starting today, you can create new EKS…

items.title: Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey now supports durability
items.snippet: Today, AWS announces durability support for Amazon ElastiCache. Durability enables you to use ElastiCache for workloads that require microsecond read latency but cannot tolerate data loss. With durability support, ElastiCache now stores data durably across…

items.snippet: Amazon Location Service today announced support for public transit and intermodal routing in the Routes API. Developers can now use the CalculateRoutes operation with two new travel modes, Transit and Intermodal, to plan journeys that combine public…

items.title: Amazon RDS for SQL Server supports Bring Your Own Media
items.snippet: Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) for SQL Server launches Bring Your Own Media (BYOM) for Microsoft SQL Server. With BYOM, customers who migrate SQL Server applications from on-premises environments can adopt a managed database service on AWS…

items.title: AWS HealthOmics now supports Nextflow version pinning at run time
items.snippet: AWS HealthOmics now allows customers to specify the Nextflow engine version at run time via the StartRun API, enabling customers to pin runs to a specific Nextflow version for controlled migration. With this launch, customers can select from supported…

items.snippet: AWS HealthOmics now supports Nextflow version 26.04, enabling customers to take advantage of new Nextflow features and enhancements: record types, the strict syntax parser, workflow output summaries, and agent logging mode. AWS HealthOmics is a HIPAA-eligible…

items.title: Get started with OpenAI GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4 models, and Codex on Amazon Bedrock
items.snippet: OpenAI frontier models GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4, and Codex, the OpenAI coding agent, are now generally available on Amazon Bedrock. Deploy frontier models on Bedrock's high performance inference engine with built-in security, governance, and pay-per-token pricing.

items.title: Building a scalable user search layer on top of Amazon Cognito
items.snippet: In this post, we show how to build a comprehensive scalable user search layer on top of Amazon Cognito using AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB, and Amazon OpenSearch Service.
